output:
# AMALGAM and FACET: Curing the Single-Virtual-Node Superposition Blind Spot in Virtual Column Networks with Identifiable, Exact-Collapse Multi-Facet Columns

## 1. Background and Current State of the Art

Graph classification maps a variable-size attributed graph to a fixed-size representation that a standard classifier can consume [1]. Historically this was done by feature engineering: hand-designed graph kernels (e.g., Weisfeiler-Lehman subtree kernels [19]) or, in cheminformatics, fixed molecular fingerprints such as the 1024-bit circular/ECFP descriptors computed by RDKit [16]. Modern graph neural networks (GNNs) instead learn the representation end to end via message passing: each node repeatedly aggregates messages from its neighbors and updates its state, after which a graph-level READOUT pools the node states into one vector [1][2][12]. The readout is the component that turns a set of node embeddings of arbitrary cardinality into the fixed-size graph embedding, and its design controls what graph-level information survives to the classifier.

The simplest readouts are permutation-invariant global pools -- sum, mean, or max over node states -- which are cheap but lossy [5][12]. More expressive readouts include order-invariant set encoders such as Set2Set [6], sorted pooling (SortPool / DGCNN) [7], differentiable hierarchical pooling that learns soft node-to-cluster assignments (DiffPool, MinCutPool) [8][9], and attention-based multiset readouts such as the Graph Multiset Transformer [10] and graph capsules (CapsGNN) [11]. A distinct and widely used device is the VIRTUAL NODE (also called the master or super node): a single extra node is added to the graph and connected to every real node, so that after message passing its state is a global summary the model can read as the graph representation; the virtual node also acts as a shortcut that lets distant nodes exchange information, and it is now a standard add-on in MPNN and OGB pipelines [2][17].

Column Networks (CLN) are a deep architecture for collective classification over attributed relational graphs [3]. CLN stacks many layers of mini-"columns" -- one column per node -- where each layer mixes a node's own hidden state with a summary of its neighbors' states and uses highway-style gating to train networks deep enough to propagate information across many hops without the oversmoothing and vanishing gradients that plague naive deep message passing [3]. CLN is precisely the node-representation engine that the source paper reuses for whole-graph representation learning.

The source paper introduces the Virtual Column Network (VCN) [4]. VCN augments an attributed graph with ONE virtual node bidirectionally connected to ALL existing nodes -- representing latent, whole-graph aspects not available from local attributes or connectivity -- and runs the expanded graph through a Column Network; the VIRTUAL NODE's learned representation IS the graph representation, and the whole model is trained end to end for classification [4][3]. On the three largest NCI BioAssay anticancer screens from PubChem (Lung Cancer, Leukemia, Yeast Anticancer), with compounds encoded as graphs (atoms as nodes, bonds as edges) and one-hot atom-feature vectors as input, and on a software-vulnerability detection task over source code, VCN outperforms baselines on AUC and F1, and its automatically learned representation beats state-of-the-art fingerprint feature engineering [4][16].

VCN's design, however, embeds an entire graph into the state of a SINGLE, uniformly connected virtual node -- one permutation-invariant global readout. Yet the labels that matter in both validation domains are frequently MULTI-LOCAL: a molecule's activity can depend on several distinct pharmacophores at different sites, and a function's vulnerability can arise from any of several distinct code patterns. The source paper anticipates this and proposes, as explicit future work, using MULTIPLE virtual nodes so that the graph is embedded into a MATRIX whose columns are virtual-node representations (one column per task for multitask learning, or one column per subgraph for large structured graphs), and trying other node-representation backbones -- Gated Graph Sequence NN [13], Graph NN [14], and diffusion-CNN [15] -- in place of Column Networks [4]. What the paper does not address is HOW multiple virtual nodes could specialize without subgraph supervision; that binding problem is exactly what this proposal targets.

## 2. Key Findings and Analysis

We name the core limitation AMALGAM. A single virtual node uniformly bidirectionally connected to all nodes computes ONE permutation-invariant global readout g = AGG({h_i}) over the node states. When a graph label depends on the PRESENCE, COUNT, CONJUNCTION, or DISJUNCTION of several independent local motifs sitting at disjoint regions, this readout SUPERPOSES their contributions into a single fixed-width vector. Under a sum/mean pool the effect is literally additive superposition: distinct motif multisets can collapse to the same pooled vector (a collision), so no downstream classifier can recover which motifs are present or how many; max pooling is no better for counts or for conjunctions across distinct channels. This is not an optimization or sample-size failure -- it is a representational impossibility of any single global pool for multi-motif labels. AMALGAM is silent on graphs with a single dominant motif (one global summary suffices) and grows with the number of co-existing discriminative substructures.

The source paper's own remedy -- "just use K virtual nodes and stack their representations into a matrix" -- does NOT escape AMALGAM, and we make the reason precise. If each of K virtual nodes is uniformly bidirectionally connected to ALL nodes, the K virtual nodes are EXCHANGEABLE: every one sees the identical neighborhood (the whole node set) and is updated by the identical symmetric rule, so the model is invariant under permuting them. By this exchange symmetry they converge to IDENTICAL embeddings; the resulting K-column matrix has all columns equal and is therefore RANK-1, carrying no more information than the single-virtual-node pool. Naively adding virtual nodes buys nothing. The real, unsolved problem is NON-DEGENERATE SPECIALIZATION -- making each virtual node bind to a DISTINCT latent aspect / subgraph -- WITHOUT any subgraph labels to supervise the binding.

We propose FACET (Facet Assignment with Columnar Exact-collapse Ties), a minimal extension INSIDE the VCN / Column-Network framework that turns the K exchangeable virtual nodes into K specialized columns. FACET replaces uniform all-to-all virtual connectivity with a DIFFERENTIABLE SOFT ASSIGNMENT: each virtual node k attends to a learned soft region/aspect of the node set (assignment weights a_{k,i} over nodes i), so the expanded-graph wiring is learned end to end with NO subgraph labels. The K virtual-node states become the COLUMNS of a non-degenerate graph matrix, aggregated by a permutation-invariant readout OVER columns. To provably break the virtual-node exchange symmetry that causes rank-1 collapse, FACET adds an explicit IDENTIFIABILITY / ANTI-COLLAPSE constraint with weight beta -- assignment load-balancing plus column decorrelation / orthogonality (or an entropy penalty) -- that pushes the columns to be distinct and high-rank, so the matrix can express disjunctive ("motif A OR motif B") and conjunctive ("A AND B") multi-motif decisions that a single pooled vector cannot.

Crucially, FACET is a strict, zero-regression extension. At the IDENTITY DEFAULT -- K = 1, assignment clamped to uniform all-to-all, and beta = 0 -- FACET's expanded graph, Column Network message passing, and column readout are bit-for-bit those of single-virtual-node VCN, so it reproduces VCN DECISION-FOR-DECISION: E_collapse = max over examples of | logit_FACET - logit_VCN | <= 1e-6, and all predicted labels match. This identity is CI-gateable: a unit test instantiates both models on shared weights at the default and FAILS the build if E_collapse > 1e-6. Hence FACET can never regress below the validated VCN baseline; specialization only adds capacity where multi-motif structure exists. The contribution occupies a four-clause novelty wedge: C1 operates WITHIN the VCN expanded-graph + Column-Network readout (same node-representation engine; drop-in readout, no new encoder); C2 replaces uniform all-to-all virtual connectivity with a differentiable soft subgraph/aspect assignment learned end to end with NO subgraph labels; C3 adds an explicit identifiability/anti-collapse constraint that provably breaks virtual-node exchange symmetry so the K columns SPECIALIZE (non-degenerate, high-rank matrix) and express multi-motif disjunctive/conjunctive decisions a single pool cannot; C4 provably EXACT-collapses to single-virtual-node VCN at the K=1 / uniform default (E_collapse <= 1e-6, CI-gateable). No prior art covers more than two of C1-C4.

## 3. Novel Insights and the New Research Idea (5-Question Formulation)

**Here is a high-level summarized insight of a research field Machine Learning.**

**Here are the five core questions:**

**[Question 1] - What is the problem?**

Can FACET -- replacing VCN's single uniformly-connected virtual node with K virtual nodes that each learn a differentiable soft assignment to a distinct subgraph/aspect of the node set, regularized by an explicit anti-collapse identifiability constraint that breaks their exchange symmetry -- recover multi-motif graph labels (presence/count/conjunction/disjunction of several independent local substructures) that VCN's single global virtual-node readout provably superposes, while provably collapsing decision-for-decision to single-virtual-node VCN at the K=1 uniform-assignment identity default (E_collapse <= 1e-6, CI-gateable) with zero regression?

**[Question 2] - Why is it interesting and important?**

P1 -- Multi-motif structure is the COMMON case in BOTH of VCN's own validation domains, so AMALGAM is a broad latent limitation, not a corner case. VCN [4] was validated on (i) NCI BioAssay anticancer bioactivity (compounds as atom/bond graphs) and (ii) software-vulnerability detection from source code [4][18]. In medicinal chemistry, biological activity is routinely driven by SEVERAL distinct pharmacophores -- spatially disjoint substructures (a hydrogen-bond donor here, an aromatic / hydrophobic feature there, a charged group elsewhere) -- and the standard ECFP / circular-fingerprint representation that VCN was shown to beat is itself a MULTISET of many independent substructure features precisely because activity is multi-local [16]. In vulnerability detection, a single function is commonly labeled vulnerable if it exhibits ANY of several distinct, independent code patterns (unchecked length leading to overflow, a tainted-source-to-sink data-flow, a use-after-free, a missing bounds guard); the Devign, Draper VDISC, and Big-Vul corpora aggregate exactly such heterogeneous CWE families, so the true label is a DISJUNCTION over disjoint code regions [18][29][30]. Both domains therefore generate labels of the form "presence / count / conjunction / disjunction of multiple motifs at disjoint sites" -- precisely the family that a single global virtual-node readout provably superposes into one inseparable vector (AMALGAM). Consequence for future research: any improvement that restores multi-motif separability is broadly applicable wherever VCN (or virtual-node GNNs generally) is deployed, not a synthetic edge case; the gap scales with the number of co-occurring discriminative substructures, which is large in real screening libraries and real codebases.

P2 -- FACET directly REALIZES the source paper's own stated "matrix of virtual-node columns" future work, and supplies the mechanism the paper left missing. The VCN paper [4] explicitly anticipates using MULTIPLE virtual nodes so the graph is embedded into a MATRIX whose columns are virtual-node representations (one column per task for multitask learning, one per subgraph for large structured graphs), and proposes swapping the Column-Network backbone for Gated Graph Sequence NN [13], the Graph NN model [14], or diffusion-CNN [15]. What the paper does NOT address is HOW multiple virtual nodes could specialize without subgraph supervision -- and we show why the naive realization fails: K virtual nodes each uniformly bidirectionally connected to ALL nodes are EXCHANGEABLE (each sees the identical neighborhood -- the whole node set -- under the identical symmetric update), so by exchange symmetry they converge to IDENTICAL embeddings and the K-column matrix is RANK-1, gaining nothing. FACET is the missing mechanism: differentiable soft assignment plus an anti-collapse identifiability constraint that provably breaks the symmetry. Consequence: this is not a tangential idea but the named, unsolved core of the paper's own roadmap; solving it converts a hand-waved future-work bullet into a provably non-degenerate construction, and the backbone-agnostic framing (C1 reuse) means the same fix carries to the [13]/[14]/[15] backbones the paper also wanted to try.

P3 -- The exact-collapse guarantee makes FACET an ADOPTABLE, zero-regression drop-in: low risk, high value. At the identity default { K=1; assignment clamped uniform all-to-all; beta=0; single-column readout = identity }, FACET's expanded graph, Column-Network message passing [3], and readout are bit-for-bit those of single-virtual-node VCN, so it reproduces VCN DECISION-FOR-DECISION with E_collapse = max_x |logit_FACET(x) - logit_VCN(x)| <= 1e-6, CI-gateable by a unit test on shared weights that fails the build if the bound is exceeded. This yields a "ship-in-shadow" adoption path: enable FACET at the identity default and prove decision-for-decision reproduction of the deployed VCN baseline, then dial up K and beta only where multi-motif structure exists. Because the worst case is literally "FACET == VCN", the method can NEVER regress below the validated baseline -- specialization only ADDS capacity. Consequence for practice: teams that already trust VCN (or the virtual-node trick in MPNN/OGB pipelines [2][17]) get a strictly safe upgrade with a machine-checkable do-no-harm contract, removing the usual deployment risk that blocks adoption of new readout architectures.

P4 -- Interpretability / mechanistic value: specialized columns plus soft assignments give per-motif, inspectable evidence. Under AMALGAM, the prediction is a single pooled vector in which the contributions of different substructures are additively superposed and unrecoverable, so the model cannot say WHICH substructure drove the call. FACET's anti-collapse constraint forces each column k to bind to a distinct facet via its assignment map a_{k,i} over real nodes, so after the fact one can read off which nodes (atoms / code tokens) each specialized column attended to and which column carried the decisive evidence -- an object-/part-centric decomposition in the spirit of slot attention [24] but inside the VCN readout. Consequence for applications: in drug discovery this surfaces the candidate pharmacophore responsible for predicted activity (a hypothesis a chemist can test or optimize), and in security triage it localizes the suspected vulnerable pattern to a code region (actionable for a reviewer), turning a black-box graph label into per-motif evidence. This inspectability is a research asset too: the rank / effective rank of the column matrix and the assignment load-balance become direct, measurable diagnostics of whether specialization actually happened.

P5 -- Generality: the move is a reusable recipe for set/multiset readouts far beyond VCN. The abstract contribution -- take a single EXCHANGEABLE, permutation-invariant readout that superposes a multiset of latent parts, replace it with K IDENTIFIABLE columns via differentiable soft assignment, and break the exchange symmetry with an explicit anti-collapse constraint, all behind an EXACT-COLLAPSE safety net to a named baseline -- is not specific to Column Networks. It applies to ANY virtual-node / master-node GNN [2][17], to global set encoders such as DeepSets [5] and Set2Set [6], and to multi-task per-column heads (the paper's "one column per task" angle, which OGB's multi-task molpcba exercises directly [17]). The load-balanced, decorrelated specialization mechanism is itself imported from a mature line -- mixture-of-experts load-balancing [22][23] and decorrelation / orthogonality regularizers [25][26] -- but it has NOT previously been combined with an exact-collapse-to-a-named-baseline contract for graph readouts. Consequence: FACET is a template ("identifiable specialized columns with a provable collapse identity") that other readout and pooling designs can reuse, which is what makes the result interesting to the broader graph-representation-learning [1] and set-learning community rather than to VCN users alone.

**[Question 3] - Why is it hard?**

We identify six obstacles O1-O6; O1 is the BINDING obstacle. For each we give the naive approach and why it fails, then why the problem is nonetheless TRACTABLE.

O1 (BINDING) -- EXCHANGE-SYMMETRY RANK-1 COLLAPSE. The paper's own future-work fix ("use K virtual nodes -> embed the graph into a matrix of columns") is a TRAP. If each of K virtual nodes is uniformly bidirectionally connected to ALL real nodes, the K virtual nodes are EXCHANGEABLE: every virtual node sees the identical neighborhood (the whole node set), is initialized from the same distribution, and is updated by the identical symmetric Column-Network rule, so the expanded-graph model is equivariant under permuting the virtual-node index. Any permutation-equivariant optimizer started from a symmetric (or symmetry-in-expectation) initialization keeps the K virtual states on the symmetric manifold v_1 = v_2 = ... = v_K; the resulting K-column matrix Z = [z_1 .. z_K] has all columns equal, so rank(Z) = 1 and it carries exactly the information of the single-virtual-node pool. Adding virtual nodes buys NOTHING. This is the BINDING obstacle because it is REPRESENTATIONAL, not a tuning/sample-size failure: no learning rate, width, depth, or amount of data escapes an exact symmetry of the hypothesis class, and every other obstacle is downstream of having first broken this symmetry. Why naive fails: "just add K virtual nodes" is the exchangeable construction (rank-1); "use K random seeds and hope SGD separates them" relies on noise to break an exact symmetry, and gradients of a symmetric loss preserve the symmetric subspace in expectation, so separation is fragile and collapses back under weight decay (the documented redundancy failure of generic multi-head readouts). Why TRACTABLE: symmetry-breaking by an explicit term is standard and provable. FACET (a) replaces uniform all-to-all wiring with a differentiable soft assignment a_{k,i} so virtual node k no longer sees the identical neighborhood (it sees an a_{k,.}-weighted view), and (b) adds an anti-collapse penalty whose gradient is non-zero precisely on the symmetric manifold and pushes off it. A penalty strictly positive on the collapsed set and zero only on a separated configuration turns "columns must differ" into an enforced constraint rather than a hope.

O2 -- SPECIALIZATION WITHOUT SUBGRAPH LABELS. We want column k to bind to a DISTINCT latent motif/aspect, but there is NO ground-truth motif or subgraph supervision in any real dataset (NCI activity labels and Devign vulnerability labels are GRAPH-level only). The assignment a_{k,i} must be learned fully unsupervised, and unsupervised assignment is UNIDENTIFIABLE without an inductive bias. Why naive fails: with no bias the assignment slides into a degenerate optimum -- (a) all mass on one node (columns ignore the rest of the graph); (b) uniform mass a_{k,i} = 1/n (every column is the global pool = AMALGAM and O1 collapse again); (c) all K columns pick the SAME region (redundant, rank-deficient). The task loss is indifferent between a specialized solution and these collapses whenever a single dominant motif already explains most labels, so plain end-to-end training drifts to collapse. Why TRACTABLE: we do not need motif labels, only an identifiability bias, which is a solved pattern. FACET imposes (i) load-balancing on the assignment (forbids all-on-one and all-columns-same-region degeneracies) and (ii) decorrelation/orthogonality on the columns Z (forbids the uniform-collapse and redundancy degeneracies) -- the same mechanism that makes DiffPool/MinCutPool soft cluster assignments and self-supervised decorrelation methods identifiable, re-aimed at virtual-node columns. Synthetic planted motifs (O5) give an oracle to VERIFY column-motif alignment without needing labels at train time.

O3 -- TENSION BETWEEN ANTI-COLLAPSE AND EXACT COLLAPSE. C3 (anti-collapse: beta>0, K>1, soft assignment that pushes columns APART) and C4 (exact collapse: at the default FACET must equal VCN BIT-FOR-BIT, E_collapse <= 1e-6) pull in opposite directions; the symmetry-breaking machinery must VANISH EXACTLY at the default, not approximately, or a floating-point residual fails the CI gate. Why naive fails: a softmax over learned logits is only APPROXIMATELY uniform at init (off by epsilon at K=1, blowing the 1e-6 budget); adding L_id with a "small" beta still perturbs the VCN solution; a separate "K=1 special case" code path is brittle. Why TRACTABLE: design the default as an ALGEBRAIC FIXED POINT, not a limit. Write the effective assignment as abar_{k,i} = (1/n) + g * (a_{k,i} - 1/n) with a scalar GATE g whose default is g = 0, so abar_{k,i} = 1/n EXACTLY (the VCN wiring) independent of the learned logits; make L_id multiply a term identically 0 when g=0 and K=1 (load-balance of a perfectly balanced assignment = 0; decorrelation of a single column = 0) and gate it by beta with default 0; use ONE code path so K=1 + g=0 + beta=0 reduces every tensor op to the VCN op by construction. The clamp is a finite set of equalities checkable symbolically and by the unit test.

O4 -- PERMUTATION-INVARIANT COLUMN READOUT WITHOUT REINTRODUCING AMALGAM. The final graph vector must be invariant to the ARBITRARY ORDER of the K columns yet must NOT re-superpose them. Why naive fails: a plain sum/mean over columns is order-invariant but is ADDITIVE SUPERPOSITION across columns -> it re-amalgamates exactly the motif contributions we just separated; max over columns discards count/conjunction signal; a fixed concatenation preserves columns but is NOT order-invariant (the model would have to learn K! orderings and the C4 clamp breaks). Why TRACTABLE: use an order-invariant-but-non-collapsing aggregator -- (i) CANONICAL-SORT-THEN-CONCATENATE (sort columns by a learned scalar key, then concatenate; invariant, lossless, and identity at K=1) or (ii) ATTENTION-OVER-COLUMNS (a small DeepSets/Set2Set/GMT-style set encoder over the K columns) regularized by the SAME decorrelation term so heads cannot collapse. Both are permutation invariant, preserve per-column identity, and reduce to "read the single column" at K=1; the choice is an ablation, not a blocker.

O5 -- CONFOUND / EVALUATION BLINDNESS. Standard pooled accuracy/AUC/F1 MARGINALIZE over motif multiplicity, and AMALGAM's harm is concentrated on the multi-motif slice, a minority of most real datasets, so on aggregate metrics the harm is largely INVISIBLE and a real improvement can be washed out. Why naive fails: reporting only dataset-level AUC/F1 (as the source paper does) cannot distinguish FACET from VCN if 80% of graphs are single-dominant-motif (where AMALGAM is silent by construction), risking a false negative. Why TRACTABLE: make the harm OBSERVABLE by construction. (i) SYNTHETIC planted-multi-motif graphs with KNOWN multiplicity and label type (presence / count(>=t) / A AND B / A OR B) give exact ground truth for the very signal AMALGAM destroys; sweeping the number of co-occurring motifs traces the gap curve directly. (ii) On real data, report MULTI-MOTIF-STRATIFIED accuracy/F1 (bin graphs by number of co-occurring discriminative substructures via annotations or substructure mining). The contribution is then measured exactly where theory predicts, and "approx 0 change on single-motif strata" becomes a checkable, falsifiable claim.

O6 -- BACKBONE / OVERSMOOTHING INTERACTION. Each virtual node is a densely connected HUB; K such hubs in a DEEP Column Network can accelerate OVERSMOOTHING (node states homogenize across layers), which both hurts node representations and, ironically, pushes columns back toward each other (re-feeding O1). Why naive fails: naively wiring K uniform all-to-all virtual nodes adds K global mixing channels that shortcut everything to a low-frequency average; going deeper or adding more virtual nodes makes it worse. Why TRACTABLE: the same soft assignment that breaks symmetry also CONTROLS mixing. A SPARSE / peaked assignment a_{k,.} (encouraged by an entropy term inside L_id) makes each virtual hub mix only its facet's nodes, not the whole graph; Column Networks are HIGHWAY-GATED by design [3], which already mitigates oversmoothing across depth, and the assignment gate g further limits virtual-node influence; effective-rank and per-layer similarity diagnostics detect oversmoothing early, and the backbone-swap ablation checks that the effect is engine-agnostic and controllable.

**[Question 4] - Why hasn't it been solved before?**

The contribution requires four clauses HELD JOINTLY, and FACET is the sole occupant of all four: C1 operates WITHIN the VCN expanded-graph + Column-Network readout (a drop-in readout module reusing the SAME node-representation engine, no new encoder); C2 replaces UNIFORM all-to-all virtual connectivity with a DIFFERENTIABLE SOFT subgraph/aspect ASSIGNMENT a_{k,i} learned end to end with NO subgraph labels; C3 adds an explicit IDENTIFIABILITY / ANTI-COLLAPSE constraint (load-balancing + column decorrelation/orthogonality) that PROVABLY breaks virtual-node exchange symmetry so the K columns SPECIALIZE (non-degenerate, high-rank), expressing disjunctive/conjunctive multi-motif decisions a single pooled vector cannot; C4 provably EXACT-collapses to single-virtual-node VCN at K=1 / uniform-assignment / beta=0 (E_collapse <= 1e-6, CI-gateable). No prior-art family clears more than TWO of C1-C4.

(1) DiffPool / MinCutPool soft-cluster hierarchical pooling [8][9]. HAS a differentiable soft node-to-cluster assignment matrix S (genuinely C2-flavored), the closest prior mechanism to FACET's assignment. MISSES C1 (it is a hierarchical-pooling layer that COARSENS the graph into fewer super-nodes for the next message-passing layer, not the VCN/Column-Network expanded-graph virtual-node readout). MISSES C4 (there is no identity default that reproduces single-virtual-node VCN; removing the pooling recovers a different un-pooled model). MISSES C3 in the FACET sense (its auxiliary terms -- DiffPool's link-prediction + entropy; MinCutPool's cut + orthogonality -- target cluster QUALITY / balanced cuts, NOT an anti-collapse identifiability bound that breaks VIRTUAL-NODE exchange symmetry). VERDICT: clears at most C2 (with a partial C3 flavor) -- two at most.

(2) Set2Set / SortPool (DGCNN) / DeepSets global readouts [5][6][7]. HAS principled permutation-invariant (or order-canonicalizing) readouts inside a GNN pipeline (a weak C1 flavor). MISSES C2 (they pool ALL nodes into ONE global summary; no per-virtual-node soft subgraph assignment). MISSES C3 (a single summary means there are NO K symmetry-broken columns; the exchange-symmetry problem does not even arise). MISSES C4 (no exact-collapse identity). VERDICT: clears at most C1 -- one.

(3) Graph Multiset Transformer (GMT) [10] -- the CLOSEST near-miss. HAS K learnable seed/query vectors that attend (multi-head attention) to node states and emit K pooled outputs -- touching C2 (learned attention to nodes) AND the multiple-columns flavor of C3. MISSES C1 (GMT is a Transformer pooling module, not inside the VCN/Column-Network framework; adopting it replaces, rather than reuses, the node-representation engine). MISSES C4 (neither framed nor proven as exact collapse to single-virtual-node VCN at an identity default; no K=1/uniform/beta=0 clamp with CI-gateable E_collapse <= 1e-6). MISSES the PROVABLE part of C3 (no explicit anti-collapse identifiability penalty PROVEN to break seed exchange symmetry; seeds distinguished only by random init plus data gradients can collapse to redundant attention maps -- the very rank-deficiency AMALGAM predicts). VERDICT: clears at most C2 + partial C3 -- two at most, and the C3 it touches is not the provable, symmetry-breaking version.

(4) Generic multi-head attention / multiple readout heads [27]. HAS K parallel heads/readouts superficially resembling K columns (a partial-C3 flavor). MISSES the provable C3 (with no decorrelation/load-balancing/identifiability constraint, heads empirically COLLAPSE to redundancy -- documented head redundancy -- so no guarantee of non-degenerate specialization; the rank-1 trap is unaddressed). MISSES C2 (plain heads are not framed as a soft SUBGRAPH/aspect assignment replacing uniform all-to-all virtual connectivity). MISSES C1 and C4 (not VCN/Column-Network-native; no exact-collapse contract). VERDICT: clears at most a partial C3 -- one (and not even the provable version).

(5) CapsGNN graph capsules [11]. HAS multiple graph-level capsule vectors produced by dynamic routing (a C3-flavored "multiple specialized vectors" idea). MISSES the provable C3 (EM/dynamic routing is an iterative agreement procedure, NOT a load-balanced soft assignment with an anti-collapse identifiability bound; no proof it breaks an exchange symmetry to force distinct, high-rank columns). MISSES C2 (routing coefficients are not a differentiable soft subgraph assignment a_{k,i} replacing uniform virtual connectivity). MISSES C1 and C4 (heavy bespoke capsule machinery, not a drop-in VCN/Column-Network readout; no exact-collapse identity). VERDICT: clears at most C3 (loosely) -- one.

(6) Master / virtual-node trick in MPNN / OGB [2][17]. HAS the virtual / master / super node connected to all real nodes, read as a global summary, native to the message-passing readout (a clean C1). MISSES C2 (connectivity is UNIFORM all-to-all; no soft per-node assignment). MISSES C3 (one or a few exchangeable uniformly-connected virtual nodes have no specialization and no anti-collapse constraint -- exactly the degenerate, rank-1 baseline AMALGAM describes). ROLE: it is the C4 EXACT-COLLAPSE ANCHOR (what FACET reduces to at the identity default), NOT a competitor. VERDICT: clears C1 only -- one.

Coverage table (X = clears, . = misses, ~ = partial/non-provable):

    Family                                   C1   C2   C3   C4   max-cleared
    (1) DiffPool / MinCutPool                 .    X    ~    .    2
    (2) Set2Set / SortPool / DeepSets         X    .    .    .    1
    (3) Graph Multiset Transformer (GMT)      .    X    ~    .    2
    (4) Multi-head attention / heads          .    .    ~    .    1
    (5) CapsGNN graph capsules                .    .    ~    .    1
    (6) Master/virtual-node (MPNN/OGB)        X    .    .    .    1  (= the C4 anchor)
    FACET (this proposal)                     X    X    X    X    4

No family clears more than TWO of C1-C4, and the two nearest (DiffPool/MinCutPool, GMT) clear their second clause only in a NON-PROVABLE, coarsening-oriented or redundancy-prone form. THE BARRIER -- why this stayed unsolved. Two field-level assumptions hid the cell. First, the community treated "add virtual nodes / add seeds / add heads" as TRIVIALLY beneficial extra capacity and never confronted the EXCHANGE-SYMMETRY RANK-1 COLLAPSE: uniformly-connected virtual nodes (or unconstrained seeds/heads) are permutation-equivalent and converge to redundant embeddings, so naively scaling K buys nothing -- a representational fact, not a tuning issue, that the "just use K columns" folklore (including the source paper's own future-work bullet [4]) silently assumed away. Even the learned-aggregation lineage the field DID develop -- attention and inductive neighbor aggregation (GAT [20], GraphSAGE [21]) -- learns per-edge or per-neighbor weights but retains a SINGLE global readout, so it never poses the virtual-node exchange-symmetry question at all. The machinery that WOULD break the symmetry -- load-balanced routing [22][23], decorrelation/orthogonality penalties [25][26], and the object-centric specialization of slot attention [24] -- lived in the mixture-of-experts and representation-learning literatures and was never imported into graph virtual-node readouts as a PROVABLE anti-collapse identifiability constraint. Second, the graph-pooling line (DiffPool, MinCutPool, GMT, CapsGNN [8][9][10][11]) optimized expressiveness, coarsening, or WL-style discrimination [12][19] on standard TU benchmarks [28], but with NO exact-collapse-to-a-named-baseline safety contract; without that contract any learned readout looks like just another attention/pooling heuristic and carries deployment risk, so no one framed the problem as "specialize K columns AND provably reduce to the deployed single-virtual-node VCN at an identity default." FACET is unlocked precisely by holding both at once: differentiable soft assignment plus a PROVABLE anti-collapse constraint (C2+C3) wired so the construction EXACT-collapses to single-virtual-node VCN at K=1/uniform/beta=0 (C4), entirely inside the VCN/Column-Network readout (C1).

**[Question 5] - What are the key components of my approach and results?**

(a) METHOD / ARCHITECTURE. Input graph G = (V, E), |V| = n, real-node input features x_i, real-node hidden states h_i in R^d produced by the Column Network (CLN) layers, unchanged from VCN [3][4]. Add K virtual nodes u_1..u_K. Instead of VCN's single virtual node uniformly connected to all real nodes, virtual node k is connected to real node i with a learned, differentiable SOFT ASSIGNMENT weight a_{k,i} in [0,1]:

    s_{k,i} = w_k^T phi(h_i)              (learned scoring of node state h_i by per-column w_k)
    a_{k,i} = softmax_i( s_{k,i} )        (normalized over real nodes i, per column k)

so each column k attends to a learned soft region/aspect ("facet") of the node set. Effective wiring with the exact-collapse gate g (scalar, default 0):

    abar_{k,i} = (1/n) + g * ( a_{k,i} - 1/n )

At g = 0, abar_{k,i} = 1/n EXACTLY = VCN's uniform all-to-all wiring, independent of the learned logits; at g = 1 the wiring is the fully learned soft assignment. Message passing is REUSED UNCHANGED: run the SAME Column-Network message passing on the expanded graph [3]; each virtual node k aggregates the assignment-weighted real-node states m_k = sum_i abar_{k,i} * h_i and updates its state z_k via the unchanged CLN/highway-gated update. FACET adds NO new encoder; it only changes WIRING and READOUT (wedge C1). Collect the K virtual-node states as columns of a graph matrix Z = [ z_1 | z_2 | ... | z_K ] in R^{d x K}; AMALGAM corresponds to rank(Z) = 1 (all columns equal), FACET aims for high-rank, specialized Z.

Anti-collapse / identifiability loss (breaks exchange symmetry, wedge C3):

    L_id = beta * ( load_balance(a) + decorrelation(Z) )     [ + optional entropy(a) ]

  - load_balance(a): penalize imbalance in per-column total mass and per-node spread, e.g. load_balance(a) = || (1/n) sum_i a_{.,i} - (1/K) 1 ||^2 plus a per-node spread term; = 0 when assignment mass is balanced across columns (forbids all-mass-on-one-node and all-columns-same-region degeneracies).
  - decorrelation(Z): with Zhat = column-centered, unit-norm columns and C = Zhat^T Zhat (K x K), decorrelation(Z) = || C - I ||_F^2 / (K*(K-1)) over off-diagonals (0 for K=1); = 0 when columns are mutually orthogonal (high rank).
  - entropy(a) (optional): -sum a_{k,i} log a_{k,i}, tuned to keep assignments peaked enough to control oversmoothing (O6) without going one-hot.

EXACT-COLLAPSE statement (wedge C4). At the identity default { K = 1; g = 0 (=> abar = uniform); beta = 0; single-column readout = identity }: abar_{1,i} = 1/n exactly so message passing is bit-for-bit VCN; load_balance of a single balanced column = 0 and decorrelation of a single column = 0 and beta = 0 so L_id == 0 IDENTICALLY; the column readout over a single column is the identity so the graph vector = z_1 = VCN's virtual-node state. Therefore E_collapse = max_x | logit_FACET(x) - logit_VCN(x) | <= 1e-6 and argmax matches for all x. All K>1, g>0, beta>0 contributions are identically 0 at this default by construction (single code path, algebraic fixed point per O3).

Permutation-invariant-but-non-amalgamating column readout (O4). Aggregate Z to the final graph vector r by an order-invariant aggregator that does NOT superpose columns: default/primary CANONICAL-SORT-THEN-CONCAT -- sort columns by a learned scalar key kappa_k = q^T z_k, then r = concat(z_{(1)}..z_{(K)}); invariant to column order, lossless, identity at K=1. Alternative: ATTENTION-OVER-COLUMNS (DeepSets/Set2Set/GMT-style set encoder over the K columns) regularized by the SAME decorrelation term so heads cannot collapse. A plain sum/mean over columns is explicitly REJECTED (re-amalgamates, O4). Total loss L = L_task( classifier(r), y ) + L_id with L_task the source paper's classification loss; train end to end; K, beta, g, readout are hyperparameters; the CLN backbone, optimizer, and feature pipeline (RDKit atom features for NCI) are unchanged from VCN to keep the comparison head-to-head [4][16].

EXACT-COLLAPSE UNIT TEST (CI gate, proves C4): instantiate VCN (single virtual node + CLN) and FACET on SHARED weights; set FACET to the identity default; for every example x in a fixed probe set assert | logit_FACET(x) - logit_VCN(x) | <= 1e-6 AND argmax_FACET(x) == argmax_VCN(x); E_collapse = max_x of that abs difference; FAIL THE BUILD if E_collapse > 1e-6. This runs in CI on every dataset.

(b) DATASETS (5-7; SYNTHETIC planted-multi-motif is PRIMARY). (1) NCI BioAssay -- Lung Cancer, Leukemia, Yeast Anticancer (PubChem) [4]: the source paper's own screens; head-to-head with VCN; atoms = nodes, bonds = edges, RDKit one-hot atom features [16]; class-balanced active/inactive; AUC, F1. (2) TU graph-classification benchmarks: D&D, PROTEINS, NCI1, NCI109, MUTAG, ENZYMES [28]: standard, directly comparable to the pooling literature. (3) OGB: ogbg-molhiv (scaffold split, AUC) and ogbg-molpcba (multitask) [17]: molhiv = harder generalization; molpcba's MULTITASK setting directly exercises the paper's "one virtual-node column per task" angle. (4) Software-vulnerability detection: Devign, Draper VDISC, Big-Vul [18][29][30]: the paper's second domain; functions contain MULTIPLE distinct vulnerable patterns => NATURAL multi-motif DISJUNCTION; F1, AUC, accuracy. (5) SYNTHETIC planted-multi-motif graphs (PRIMARY DISCRIMINATOR): plant K distinct motifs at DISJOINT locations on a random-graph backbone with CONTROLLABLE multiplicity; label families PRESENCE (control, AMALGAM mostly silent), COUNT(>= t) (requires counting -> AMALGAM fails), CONJUNCTION (A AND B, cross-channel -> AMALGAM fails), DISJUNCTION (A OR B, mirrors multi-pattern vulnerability); SWEEP the number M of co-occurring motifs (M = 1,2,3,4,6,8) to TRACE the AMALGAM-vs-FACET gap curve; ground-truth motif locations enable motif-to-column alignment diagnostics.

(c) BASELINES. single-virtual-node VCN (the EXACT-COLLAPSE ANCHOR [4][3]); naive K-virtual-node VCN (uniform all-to-all, NO assignment, NO anti-collapse -- the predicted RANK-1 COLLAPSE control); FACET-minus-anti-collapse (soft assignment present but beta = 0 -- isolates the necessity of the anti-collapse term); FACET (full); GIN + sum/mean/max [12]; Set2Set [6]; SortPool/DGCNN [7]; DiffPool [8]; MinCutPool [9]; Graph Multiset Transformer (GMT) [10] (closest near-miss); CapsGNN [11]; ECFP/RDKit 1024-bit + SVM and RF [16] (the feature baseline VCN beat).

(d) METRICS. Primary (match source paper): AUC, F1, accuracy. E_collapse: max abs logit difference at the identity default, CI-gated <= 1e-6 -> PROVES C4. MULTI-MOTIF-STRATIFIED accuracy/F1: stratified by number M of co-occurring motifs and by label type {presence, count, AND, OR}, on synthetic (exact ground truth) and on real data where motif annotations / mined substructures exist -- THIS is where AMALGAM vs FACET separates, and "approx 0 change on single-motif strata" is a checkable claim. SPECIALIZATION DIAGNOSTICS (evidence C3 works): effective rank of Z (e.g., exp(entropy of normalized singular values)) -- approx 1 under AMALGAM/naive-K-VCN, grows under FACET; column decorrelation || C - I ||_F (off-diagonal); assignment load-balance and entropy; qualitative MOTIF-TO-COLUMN alignment (do columns align with planted motifs / known pharmacophores / vulnerable patterns?).

(e) ABLATIONS. K in {1, 2, 4, 8, 16} (K=1 must hit the exact-collapse anchor); beta sweep 0 -> on (0, 1e-3, 1e-2, 1e-1, 1) to DEMONSTRATE anti-collapse NECESSITY (effective rank of Z and stratified accuracy should rise with beta then saturate); assignment type UNIFORM (= naive-K-VCN, rank-1 control) vs SOFT learned a_{k,i}; column-readout choice (canonical-sort-concat vs attention-over-columns vs the rejected sum, showing the sum re-amalgamates); backbone swap CLN -> GGS-NN [13] / Graph NN [14] / diffusion-CNN [15] (showing C1 reuse is ENGINE-AGNOSTIC within the VCN frame).

(f) FALSIFIABLE PREDICTIONS / EXPECTED OUTCOMES. P1 (multiplicity plateau): on SYNTHETIC count(>=t) / CONJUNCTION / DISJUNCTION labels, as M grows (M >= 2), single-virtual-node VCN and naive-K-VCN PLATEAU near chance-for-multiplicity (stratified accuracy approx 0.5-0.6 on multi-motif strata) while FACET reaches HIGH accuracy (approx 0.90+ on the same strata) and the gap WIDENS monotonically with M; REFUTED IF FACET fails to exceed naive-K-VCN by >= 0.20 stratified accuracy at M >= 3, OR if single-node VCN already solves count/AND/OR. P2 (rank growth is the mechanism): under FACET the EFFECTIVE RANK of Z GROWS with beta and K (toward min(d, K)) while under naive-K-VCN it stays approx 1 regardless of K; REFUTED IF naive-K-VCN already shows effective rank >> 1, OR if FACET's accuracy gains occur WITHOUT any rise in effective rank. P3 (real multi-motif strata, silent elsewhere): on NCI (Lung Cancer / Leukemia / Yeast) and Devign/Big-Vul, FACET improves F1/AUC on the MULTI-MOTIF strata by approx +2 to +6 points (dataset-dependent) with approx 0 change (|delta| <= 0.5 point) on the SINGLE-motif strata; REFUTED IF FACET REGRESSES on single-motif strata OR shows no multi-motif-stratified gain over VCN. P4 (exact collapse holds everywhere): at the identity default E_collapse <= 1e-6 on EVERY dataset with argmax matching VCN for all x; REFUTED (C4 falsified) IF E_collapse > 1e-6 on ANY dataset/probe -- a hard build failure. Headline: the synthetic planted-multi-motif sweep is the PRIMARY discriminator (it makes the exact signal AMALGAM destroys observable and shows FACET recovering it while VCN/naive-K-VCN plateau); effective-rank diagnostics confirm the symmetry-breaking MECHANISM; real-data stratified gains with zero single-motif regression plus a green E_collapse CI gate close the loop on C1-C4.

(g) RESPONSIBLE-AI CONSIDERATIONS (advisory; integrated from the team's RAI review, verdict YELLOW with no blocking issues -- all foldable without changing the method). F1 Dual-use in vulnerability detection: per-column localization of vulnerable patterns is inherently dual-use; adopt coordinated-disclosure framing, evaluate only on already-public/already-patched CVEs and standard benchmarks (Devign, Big-Vul, Draper VDISC), measure defender-oriented triage value (recall at fixed review budget) rather than exploit generation, and do not release exploit-ready line-level localizations or weights tuned to surface undisclosed bugs. F2 Interpretability over-trust: per-column "motif evidence" is an attribution, not a causal guarantee; report calibrated uncertainty, run faithfulness checks (deletion/insertion, sufficiency, model-randomization sanity tests), frame outputs as decision support requiring human-in-the-loop, and document column entanglement near the identity default. F3 Data governance / licensing / secret scrubbing: cite and respect each dataset's license (OGB, TUDataset, PubChem/NCI, Devign/Big-Vul/Draper), share splits/indices rather than re-hosting data where required, confirm no PII, and scrub secrets/credentials from code corpora before training. F4 Fairness / disaggregation: report performance disaggregated by motif multiplicity and label logic and by data subgroup (scaffold/chemotype for molecules; language/project/CWE for code), making the multi-motif slice a first-class result. F5 Safety of the exact-collapse gate (GREEN, endorsed): make E_collapse <= 1e-6 a required, audited, fail-closed CI check with pinned tolerance, seeds, and probe set, logged as zero-regression evidence. F6 Reproducibility / claims hygiene: use scaffold splits for molecules and time/project splits for code, release seeds and configs, report mean +/- std, phrase predictions as falsifiable ranges, and avoid claiming the model "understands/discovers" a motif. F7 Bioactivity non-clinical framing: label anticancer outputs as in-silico screening hypotheses requiring wet-lab validation and human-in-the-loop, make no clinical/efficacy/dosing claim, and note the primary-screen reproducibility caveats.

Six non-arXiv-native works are cited by author-year-title-venue with no arXiv ID asserted ([7] SortPool/DGCNN, AAAI 2018; [11] CapsGNN, ICLR 2019 OpenReview; [14] Scarselli GNN, IEEE TNN 2009; [16] ECFP, J. Chem. Inf. Model. 2010, DOI:10.1021/ci100050t; [19] WL graph kernels, JMLR 2011; [30] Big-Vul, MSR 2020, DOI:10.1145/3379597.3387501). No fabricated identifiers: one search-engine-hallucinated arXiv ID for Big-Vul was detected and rejected in favor of its real MSR 2020 record.
